paper_title: Managerial human resource quality of firms in the Mekong Delta, Vietnam

entities.id: College of Economics, Can Tho University
entities.description: Academic institution where the authors are affiliated.
The primary research field of the paper.
The core problem examining the professional knowledge, experience, and training of firm managers.
A theory developed by Hambrick and Mason (1986) used to examine the impact of human resources on firm growth.
Statistical method used for hypothesis testing and comparing means.
Analysis of variance used to analyze the differences among group means.
Method of primary data collection involving 450 firms.
Digital data collected directly from firms in the Mekong Delta.
Data provided by relevant organizations, including the General Statistical Office of Vietnam.
Measurement of sales performance for firms in the region.
Educational qualification metric showing the percentage of managers lacking a degree.

entities.id: Average amount of bribes (VND 115.7 million per year)
entities.description: Metric measuring the cost of business environment constraints.

entities.id: Number of firms in Mekong Delta 2014 (31,146)
entities.description: The total count of firms in the research region.
